title: Festivals & events
subtitle: 41 celebrations through the year — pick a day to see what's on.

festivals.name: New Year's Day
festivals.description: National public holiday marking the start of the new year with celebrations across Mexico.

festivals.name: Epiphany (Three Kings Day)
festivals.description: Religious holiday celebrated across Mexico with parades, processions, and the traditional Rosca de Reyes cake shared among families.

festivals.name: Carnival of Mazatlán
festivals.description: Mexico's largest and most vibrant carnival featuring elaborate floats, live music, dancing, and parades along the waterfront.

festivals.name: Carnival of Veracruz
festivals.description: One of Mexico's most famous carnivals with colorful costumes, dancing, and Caribbean-influenced music and traditions.

festivals.name: Carnival of Cozumel
festivals.description: Island carnival celebration with colorful parades, live music, and dancing that blends Caribbean and Maya traditions.

festivals.name: Festival de la Canción de Viña del Mar (Mexico Border)
festivals.description: Regional music festival celebrating Latin American song traditions with performances from local and international artists.

festivals.name: Fiesta de San Blas
festivals.description: Traditional festival honoring Saint Blas with processions, religious services, and local cultural celebrations in this coastal town.

festivals.name: Constitution Day
festivals.description: National public holiday commemorating the adoption of the Mexican Constitution with civic ceremonies and parades.

festivals.name: Fiesta de la Candelaria
festivals.regionName: Central Highlands (Zacatecas, Aguascalientes, San Luis Potos
festivals.description: Religious festival celebrated nationwide but especially vibrant in Aguascalientes, featuring processions and traditional Candlemas Day customs.

festivals.name: Fiesta de Marzo (San José)
festivals.description: Spring festival celebrating Saint Joseph with processions, traditional food, and family gatherings in colonial towns.

festivals.name: Benito Juárez's Birthday
festivals.description: National public holiday honoring the birth of Mexico's most celebrated president with civic events and celebrations.

festivals.name: Equinox Celebration at Chichen Itza
festivals.description: Ancient Maya astronomical event where the sun casts a shadow shaped like a serpent on the pyramid, attracting thousands of visitors.

festivals.name: Fiesta de Semana Santa
festivals.description: Holy Week celebration across Mexico with religious processions, reenactments, and traditional ceremonies in churches nationwide.

festivals.name: Feria Nacional de San Marcos
festivals.regionName: Central Highlands (Zacatecas, Aguascalientes, San Luis Potos
festivals.description: Mexico's largest fair featuring rodeos, concerts, livestock shows, traditional food, and carnival rides throughout the month.

festivals.name: Fiesta de la Santa Cruz
festivals.regionName: Central Highlands (Zacatecas, Aguascalientes, San Luis Potos
festivals.description: Religious festival celebrated nationwide honoring the Holy Cross with processions, music, and traditional celebrations.

festivals.name: Labor Day
festivals.description: National public holiday honoring workers with parades, demonstrations, and celebrations throughout Mexico.

festivals.name: Fiesta del Corpus Christi
festivals.description: Religious festival celebrated with traditional indigenous ceremonies, colorful altars, and processions in southern Mexico.

festivals.name: Fiesta de San Juan Bautista
festivals.description: Traditional celebration honoring Saint John the Baptist with water-related festivities and religious ceremonies, especially in Yucatan.

festivals.name: Festival Guelaguetza
festivals.description: UNESCO-recognized festival showcasing Oaxacan indigenous music, dance, crafts, and traditions with spectacular cultural performances.

festivals.name: Fiesta de Santiago Apóstol
festivals.description: Traditional celebration honoring Saint James with rodeos, charreadas, traditional music, and cultural events.

festivals.name: Festival de Cine en Guadalajara
festivals.description: Latin America's largest film festival featuring international and Mexican cinema with screenings, premieres, and industry events.

festivals.name: Fiesta de Nuestra Señora de Guadalupe
festivals.description: Mexico's most important religious celebration honoring the Virgin of Guadalupe with pilgrimages, processions, and festivities nationwide.

festivals.name: Tianguis Turístico
festivals.description: Mexico's largest tourism marketplace and festival featuring cultural performances, food, crafts, and travel industry presentations.

festivals.name: Fiesta de los Muertos (Day of the Dead)
festivals.description: UNESCO world cultural heritage celebration honoring deceased loved ones with colorful ofrendas, traditional food, and cemetery gatherings.

festivals.name: Cervantino Festival
festivals.description: Mexico's premier cultural festival featuring international theatre, music, dance, and arts performances in historic colonial settings.

festivals.name: Fiesta de Octubre
festivals.description: Guadalajara's month-long celebration featuring concerts, exhibitions, sporting events, and cultural activities throughout the city.

festivals.name: Día de Muertos in Michoacán
festivals.description: Traditional Day of the Dead celebration famous for indigenous ceremonies, marigold decorations, and visits to island cemeteries.

festivals.name: Feria de Texcoco
festivals.description: Traditional fair featuring rodeos, agricultural exhibitions, crafts, and regional food celebrating rural culture.

festivals.name: Feria Nacional del Pulque
festivals.regionName: Central Highlands (Zacatecas, Aguascalientes, San Luis Potos
festivals.description: Festival celebrating Mexico's traditional fermented agave drink with tastings, cultural performances, and cultural exhibitions.

festivals.name: Feria del Queso y Vino
festivals.regionName: Central Highlands (Zacatecas, Aguascalientes, San Luis Potos
festivals.description: Food festival celebrating local cheese and wine production with tastings, cooking demonstrations, and regional cuisine.

festivals.name: Encuentro Internacional Cervantino
festivals.description: World-class performing arts festival in a UNESCO World Heritage city with theatre, music, dance, and international performances.

festivals.name: Festival de Playa de Carmen
festivals.description: Beach festival celebrating Caribbean culture with live music, dance performances, water sports, and regional cuisine.

festivals.name: Fiesta de Santa Lucia
festivals.description: Traditional celebration honoring Saint Lucy with religious services and cultural events in Baja California communities.

festivals.name: Feria de la Navidad en Oaxaca
festivals.description: Christmas season celebration in Oaxaca featuring traditional handicrafts, indigenous textiles, food, and festive decorations.

festivals.name: Posadas (Las Posadas)
festivals.description: Traditional Christmas celebration reenacting Mary and Joseph's journey to Bethlehem with processions and community gatherings.

festivals.name: Independence Day
festivals.description: Mexico's most important national holiday celebrating independence from Spain with parades, fireworks, and celebrations nationwide.

festivals.name: Batalla de Puebla Commemoration
festivals.description: National holiday commemorating Mexico's victory against French forces with civic ceremonies and cultural celebrations.

festivals.name: Fiesta de San Antonio de Padua
festivals.description: Religious celebration honoring Saint Anthony with processions, traditional food, and community gatherings in Baja California.

festivals.name: Fiesta de las Flores en Xoximilco
festivals.description: Festival celebrating the traditions of Xochimilco's floating gardens with flower arrangements, boat processions, and regional music.

festivals.name: Feria del Mezcal
festivals.description: Festival celebrating Oaxaca's famous mezcal spirits with tastings, traditional roasting demonstrations, and cultural performances.

festivals.name: Feria Internacional de la Plata
festivals.description: International silver fair celebrating Mexico's mining heritage with exhibitions, jewelry showcases, and cultural events.
